intro:
There is limited information on Tabernaemontana dakgleiensis, so I will provide information on a similar plant, Tabernaemontana divaricata, also known as the "creamy-candles" or "yellow bignay" plant. 

Tabernaemontana divaricata is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its distinctive white, fragrant flowers and its use in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana divaricata include alkaloids, such as voacangine and ajmalicine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be made into a tincture or syrup for use in herbal remedies.
